# Project Stakeholder Register

## Project: [Project Name from Context]
## Date: [Current Date]
## Version: 1.0

## 1. Executive and Governance Stakeholders

This section lists high-level stakeholders with significant decision-making **Power/Influence** over the project's direction, funding, and ultimate success.

| ID | Name/Role | Organization | Title / Function | Power/Influence | Interest | Engagement Strategy (PMBOK 7) |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :---: | :---: | :--- |
| G-01 | e.g., CEO, CIO, Head of Product (Executive Sponsor) | [Org. Name] | Executive sponsor for technology and enterprise adoption | High | High | Manage Closely: Required for funding, approvals, and removing high-level impediments. |
| G-02 | e.g., CISO, Head of Legal, Compliance Officer (Governance) | [Org. Name] | Responsible for security posture, compliance, and risk sign-off | High | High | Collaborate / Keep Satisfied: Critical for compliance, security standards, and risk sign-off. |
| G-03 | Project Sponsor (Business Sponsor) | [Org. Name] | Primary business owner; approves scope and value acceptance | High | High | Manage Closely: Final decision-maker on scope and value acceptance. |
| G-04 | Finance / Budget Owner | [Org. Name] | Finance lead controlling budget and financial benefits tracking | Medium | Medium | Keep Informed: Monitors budget adherence and tracks realization of financial benefits. |

## 2. Project Team and Execution Stakeholders

This section lists the key roles directly responsible for delivering the project's outcomes.

| ID | Name/Role | Organization | Title / Function | Power/Influence | Interest | Engagement Strategy (PMBOK 7) |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :---: | :---: | :--- |
| T-01 | Project Manager | [Org. Name] | Day-to-day project execution, stakeholder coordination, risk management | High | High | Lead & Empower: Responsible for all project execution and communication. |
| T-02 | e.g., Solutions Architect, Lead Developer (Technical Lead) | [Org. Name] | Defines architecture and integration approach; owns NFRs and technical decisions | Medium | High | Collaborate: Key in defining the technical solution and resolving integration issues. |
| T-03 | e.g., Product Owner, Business Analyst (BA/PO) | [Org. Name] | Owns backlog, acceptance criteria, and requirements traceability | Medium | High | Collaborate: Responsible for scope definition (requirements) and value maximization. |
| T-04 | e.g., QA Lead, Test Manager (Quality Assurance) | [Org. Name] | Test strategy, automation, and validation of deliverables | Low | Medium | Consult: Involved in ensuring quality standards and validating deliverables. |

## 3. Operations, End-User, and External Stakeholders

This section lists the individuals or groups who will use, maintain, or be affected by the final project deliverable.

| ID | Name/Role | Organization | Title / Function | Power/Influence | Interest | Engagement Strategy (PMBOK 7) |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :---: | :---: | :--- |
| U-01 | e.g., Customer Support Team, Sales Team (End Users) | [Org. Name] | Primary users who create/consume generated documents and templates | Low | High | Keep Informed / Consult: Directly affected by new processes/systems; provides key UAT feedback. |
| U-02 | e.g., DevOps Engineer, IT Operations Lead (Operations/SRE) | [Org. Name] | Owns deployment, monitoring, incident response and post-handover support | Medium | High | Keep Satisfied: Owns the deliverable post-handover; requires early input on support and maintenance. |
| U-03 | e.g., Key Vendor Representative, Integration Partner (External) | [External Org/Vendor] | Vendor/partner providing connectors, licensing, or integration support | Medium | Medium | Monitor / Consult: May provide dependencies (licensing, integration); monitor contractual adherence. |

---

## 4. Stakeholder Engagement Planning Summary

### 4.1 Stakeholder Power/Interest Matrix & Engagement Summary

This section translates the Power/Interest assessment into a guide for the project team. List specific stakeholders (Name/Role/ID) under each quadrant and include short, actionable Key Actions.

| Quadrant | Power/Influence | Interest | Engagement Strategy | Key Actions |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Manage Closely** | High | High | Maximum Effort: Actively manage expectations and collaborate in decision-making. | List specific stakeholders (e.g., G-01: CIO; G-03: Project Sponsor). Actions: Weekly steering meetings, joint risk reviews, decision logs. |
| **Keep Satisfied** | High | Low | Sufficient Effort: Do enough to keep them happy, but do not over-engage; ensure their key requirements are met. | List specific stakeholders (e.g., G-04: Finance). Actions: Monthly financial briefings, early cost-impact alerts. |
| **Keep Informed** | Low | High | Regular Communication: Keep them in the loop through status reports; involve them in low-impact issues. | List specific stakeholders (e.g., T-02: Solutions Architect, T-03: PO). Actions: Sprint demos, release notes, training sessions. |
| **Monitor** | Low | Low | Minimum Effort: Monitor for shifts in interest or power; rely on generic status updates. | List specific stakeholders (e.g., T-04: QA, U-03: Vendors). Actions: Periodic check-ins; escalate on change. |

### 4.2 Initial Communication Needs

- **Key Information Requirement:** What is the single most important piece of information this stakeholder needs? (e.g., "CISO needs weekly reports on security controls compliance.")
- **Preferred Communication Method:** Email, Formal Status Meeting, Ad-hoc 1-on-1, Dashboard Access.

Provide a short row for each key stakeholder (G-01..U-03) describing their Key Information Requirement and Preferred Communication Method.

---

## Instructions for Organizational Completion

- Completion guidance: Use this register as a living document. Review and update at least once per phase or iteration. Replace role-based entries with named contacts as assignments are made.
- PM alignment: Use the Engagement Strategy column to drive the Project Communications Management Plan; create meeting cadences, owners, templates and dashboard access accordingly.
- Requirements capture: Ensure `T-03 (Product Owner / BA)` captures `U-01 (End Users)` needs into the Requirements Management Plan and backlog with measurable acceptance criteria.

Fields to add (per stakeholder row): Full name, Email, Phone, Delegated representative, Decision authority level, Escalation path, Contract owner (for vendors), Last updated (date), Notes / Rationale for inference.

For inferred stakeholders: Mark the entry as **Inferred** and add a one-line justification for the inference (e.g., "Inferred — Confluence integration implies Confluence admin involvement").

---

## Deliverables & Next Steps

- Add named contacts and contact details for each stakeholder.
- Sync the Engagement Strategy to the Project Communications Plan and assign owners for each communication cadence.
- Optional outputs: 1) CSV export of this register with the completion fields above; 2) Draft Communications Plan (cadence, templates, owners).
